summary: Add 'todo install-hooks' that drops a prepare-commit-msg hook auto-prefixing todo:<id>
description:
Add a `todo install-hooks` command that writes a `prepare-commit-msg` git
hook into the current repo's .git/hooks/.

Behaviour of the installed hook:
- On commit, read current branch via `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD`.
- If branch matches `todo/<id>`, prepend `todo:<id> — ` to the commit
  message unless the message already starts with `todo:<id>` (idempotent).
- Skip merge commits, squash commits, rebases (check the commit source
  argument: only act when it is empty or "template").
- Never block the commit; on any error, exit 0 silently so a buggy hook
  can't strand the user.

`todo install-hooks`:
- Idempotent: if a Todo-managed hook already exists (detect via a sentinel
  line in the file), overwrite. If a non-Todo hook exists, refuse with
  --force flag offered.
- Print where it wrote the hook and the sentinel used.

Done contract: tests covering (a) hook content correct, (b) reinstall is
idempotent, (c) refuses to clobber unmanaged hook without --force,
(d) end-to-end: install + commit on todo/<id> branch produces correctly-
prefixed message; commit on main passes through unchanged.

resolution.note: install-hooks command lands a managed prepare-commit-msg hook with sentinel-based idempotency. 14 new tests, full suite 141 passing.
